evidence_quote: If he had not gone into Duskendale to rescue Aerys from Lord Darklyn's dungeons, the king might well have died there as Tywin Lannister sacked the town.
asserted_relation: The Defiance of Duskendale took place at Duskendale

evidence_quote: I once brought the queen's father safely out of Duskendale, where he was being held captive by a rebel lord
asserted_relation: Aerys II Targaryen was imprisoned at Duskendale (victim/captive) during the Defiance

evidence_quote: Brought King Aerys II to safety during the Defiance of Duskendale, despite an arrow wound in the chest.
asserted_relation: Barristan Selmy single-handedly rescued Aerys from Duskendale (the signature act)

evidence_quote: Denys Darklyn, Lord of Duskendale, wished to halt this decline. He desired to win a charter for Duskendale … Infuriated at Tywin's refusal, Denys devised a new plan
asserted_relation: Denys Darklyn instigated the Defiance by imprisoning Aerys (the defiant lord, primary agent)

evidence_quote: He had first heard those words from Tywin Lannister outside the walls of Duskendale. He gave me a day to bring out Aerys. Unless I returned with the king by dawn of the following day, he would take the town with steel and fire, he told me.
asserted_relation: Tywin Lannister commanded the siege force outside Duskendale as Hand of the King

evidence_quote: The only exception was young Dontos Hollard, whose life was spared when Ser Barristan Selmy requested it. Aerys, unable to refuse the man who had saved his life, granted this boon.
asserted_relation: Dontos Hollard was a victim/survivor of the post-Defiance executions, spared only by Barristan's plea

evidence_quote: Denys's wife, Lady Serala, had her tongue and womanly parts torn out, after which she was burned alive.
asserted_relation: Serala of Myr was executed in the aftermath of the Defiance

evidence_quote: Ser Jon Hollard, who had been married to Denys's sister, died together with his wife and young son.
asserted_relation: Jon Hollard was executed in the Defiance's aftermath

evidence_quote: Robin Hollard, the squire who had pulled Aerys's beard when he had first been seized, died upon the rack.
asserted_relation: Robin Hollard was tortured to death in the Defiance's aftermath

evidence_quote: the king demanded the deaths of Denys and his immediate family, as well as his uncles, aunts, and distant House Darklyn kinsmen living in Duskendale.
asserted_relation: House Darklyn was attainted and executed collectively in the Defiance's aftermath

evidence_quote: House Hollard, Lord Denys's goodkin, were attainted and executed as well.
asserted_relation: House Hollard was attainted and nearly extinguished in the Defiance's aftermath
